## 2024-07-13 - [Secure File Permissions for Memory Profiles]
**Vulnerability:** Memory profile snapshots and flamegraphs generated by `File::create` without explicit mode restrictions result in world-readable files on Unix systems, potentially exposing sensitive stack traces and memory layout information.
**Learning:** System profiling tools that dump runtime memory/stack data must enforce restrictive file permissions (e.g., `0o600`) to prevent information disclosure to unauthorized users on multi-user systems.
**Prevention:** Use `std::fs::OpenOptions` combined with `std::os::unix::fs::OpenOptionsExt::mode` to explicitly set file modes to `0o600` when creating files containing sensitive runtime data.
